medications.drug_name: mechanical haemostasis (firm direct pressure + protective non-adherent/alginate dressing)
medications.indication_rationale: Control the bleeding and protect the fragile lesion; keep the dressing intact/dry until the surgical appointment.
The definitive step — complete excision for histology; arrange within ~1-2 weeks.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the dressing intact and dry, don't pick/inspect or wet the lesion, and apply firm pressure if it re-bleeds.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents re-bleeding and protects the lesion before excision.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Prioritise and attend the urgent specialist appointment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Timely excision is the single most important step.

patient_education_points.point: This mole has several warning features and needs to be taken seriously — I can't tell from a photo whether it's a skin cancer, so it must be removed whole and tested under the microscope, urgently.
The bleeding is because the surface is fragile; keep the dressing on and press firmly if it bleeds again. The key step is the urgent surgical appointment to remove it — please prioritise that call.
If it bleeds heavily and soaks through the dressing and won't stop after 20 minutes of firm pressure, go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.interval: 48 hours (confirm specialist appointment booked); excision within ~1-2 weeks
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: That the fast-track excisional-biopsy appointment is booked; provide psychological support; full skin check.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Uncontrolled bleeding despite 20 minutes of firm pressure — ED.

must_include_items: Recognise high suspicion of melanoma (ABCDE + evolution + ugly-duckling + bleeding) and avoid false reassurance
Arrange an urgent fast-track EXCISIONAL-biopsy referral (not shave/partial, not watchful waiting)
Honest communication + psychological support and a full skin check
acceptable_alternatives: Direct urgent dermatology vs surgical referral depending on local pathways
errors_of_commission: Recommending a shave/partial biopsy of a suspected melanoma
Delaying referral for a routine appointment
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: MUST-NOT-MISS CANCER: failing to arrange an urgent excisional-biopsy pathway (or falsely reassuring / recommending a shave biopsy) is a critical failure.
